incidentSummary: Ledger has reported multiple instances of fake Telegram groups impersonating official support channels, tricking users into sharing seed phrases and sending funds to scammer addresses. These groups often use similar profile pictures and names with slight misspellings to appear legitimate.
urgency: Users are actively being deceived into surrendering private keys and funds in real-time through these impersonated groups.

incidentSummary: Attackers registered domains such as uniswop.io and uniswapx.xyz to mimic the official Uniswap interface and trick users into connecting wallets to malicious smart contracts. Multiple users lost funds after interacting with these fake sites.
urgency: These domains remain active and continue to harvest user wallet signatures and funds.

incidentSummary: Coinbase has documented cases of attackers spoofing noreply@coinbase.com to send phishing emails impersonating account verification or security alerts, directing users to fake login pages. These emails often include urgent language to prompt immediate action.
urgency: Users are receiving active phishing emails that mimic Coinbase branding and domains with high fidelity.

incidentSummary: Multiple cloned websites impersonating Glossier were discovered selling counterfeit products. Fake Instagram accounts also impersonated the brand, directing customers to fraudulent storefronts that accepted payments but never delivered authentic products.
urgency: Counterfeit stores are actively siphoning revenue and damaging brand trust.

incidentSummary: Allbirds faced widespread cloning of their website with lookalike domains selling counterfeit wool footwear. The company issued public warnings and worked with legal teams to take down numerous fraudulent sites that mimicked their branding.
urgency: Knockoff operations are eroding brand value and customer trust at scale.

incidentSummary: Brooklinen discovered multiple cloned websites using their product images and branding to sell inferior counterfeit bedding products. The company actively warned customers via social media about fraudulent sites mimicking their domain name variations.
urgency: Counterfeit sellers are actively using their product photography to steal customers.

incidentSummary: Attackers registered lookalike domains such as shopify-login.xyz and shopify-app.xyz to harvest merchant credentials. One domain redirected users to a fake login page mimicking Shopify's admin interface.
urgency: Merchant credentials are actively being harvested via lookalike login pages.

incidentSummary: Impersonators created fake Instagram and Twitter accounts using names like @revolut_support and @revolut_official to solicit crypto and personal data. Revolut confirmed over 200 fraudulent accounts were actively scamming users in Q1 2023.
source: Revolut fraud alerts (March 2023) and Financial Times
urgency: Over 200 fake social accounts are actively soliciting user data right now.

incidentSummary: A cloned online store at gymshark-outlet.com sold counterfeit apparel with fake checkout pages. The site mimicked Gymshark's branding and offered deep discounts to lure customers, leading to chargebacks and brand confusion.
source: Gymshark public notice (October 2022) and Trustpilot fraud reports
urgency: Active counterfeit storefronts are processing payments and damaging brand reputation.

incidentSummary: Attackers spoofed support@plaid.com to send phishing emails impersonating integration updates, tricking developers into revealing API keys. One victim reported receiving a fake Plaid Integration Renewal email with malicious attachments.
source: Plaid Security Blog (August 2022) and Mandiant threat intelligence
urgency: Active email spoofing campaigns are targeting developers with API key theft.
